task_completion.description: Create the 2D half-profile sketch of the bolt
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch in SolveSpace showing a closed profile with a horizontal top line, a small vertical drop, an angled line inwards, a long vertical line downwards, and a horizontal line back to the vertical axis.

task_completion.description: Revolve the half-profile 360 degrees to create the 3D bolt body
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D solid model of a countersunk bolt (flat circular top, angled conical section, and a straight cylindrical shaft) visible in the SolveSpace workspace.

task_completion.description: Sketch a hexagon on the top face of the bolt
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D hexagonal sketch centered on the top flat circular face of the bolt head.

task_completion.description: Extrude/cut the hexagonal socket into the bolt head
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D view showing the hexagonal shape recessed (cut) into the top face of the bolt head, resembling an Allen key socket.
The SolveSpace window title bar showing 'countersunk_bolt.slvs' or a save dialog saving the file to /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/.

task_completion.description: Export the model as an STL file
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog, file manager, or terminal window showing 'countersunk_bolt.stl' being saved or existing in /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/.

integrity.description: Agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the model
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace interface with active toolbars, property panels, and progressive modeling steps rather than just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Agent actually modeled the part step-by-step
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show intermediate steps like 2D sketching, dimensioning, and 3D operations, proving the model was not simply downloaded or copied.
The application being used is clearly SolveSpace, identifiable by its distinct UI, property browser, and graphics window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected model is a countersunk bolt with a total length of 36.0 mm and a maximum head diameter of 16.0 mm. The top face has a hexagonal cut (Allen key socket) with a 3.0 mm radius and 3.5 mm depth. The final files must be saved as 'countersunk_bolt.slvs' and 'countersunk_bolt.stl' in the /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/ directory.
